author: @Modrzew1988
text:
Info for people who don't know the books and don't fully realise how Netflix is butchering The Witcher. The books are basically ready-made scripts, written in a very cinematic style. All they had to do was stick to the books, which tell a very interesting and coherent story full of amazing characters.
 
And the dialogues, man, if there's anything worth remembering in pop culture, it's the dialogues created by Sapkowski. Some of them are just incredible. 

The simplified structure of The Witcher Saga (five books) looks something like this: Geralt and Yennefer are trying to raise Ciri, a princess and the only living heir to the throne of Cintra, a country conquered by Nilfgaard, they want to give her some kind of future. Geralt trains her in combat, while Yennefer together with Triss Merigold tries to give her a more general education and teach her magic. They all form a strong bond with her, but are then separated by violent events in the world of The Witcher (the rebellion on Thanedd). 

For most of the saga (basically 4 out of 5 books), Ciri, Geralt and Yen are separated and search for each other, trying to reunite. Ciri is also being searched for by the Emperor of Nilfgaard and Vilgefortz, a powerful mage. During this time, Geralt and Ciri are equally the main characters of the books, as is Yennefer, but she is a little less prominent throughout the plot. Most of the time, we follow the action by jumping between the three main characters. We spend the most time with Geralt and his company, which forms during the story. 

We are currently in the fourth season of the series and so far we know nothing, there is nothing from the core of the story. After four seasons, we do not know Vilgefortz's motivation, we do not even know yet that he wants to find Ciri and why, we do not know what the emperor wants to achieve, many important storylines have been removed and cannot be added back, but we have added some new ones that are completely irrelevant to the main story (monoliths, portals with dinosaurs coming out of them, Yennefer making a pact with some demon,etc.). For me, the series is going nowhere, and I am not surprised that Cavill left because, as a fan of the books, he probably saw it too.

author: @CpyrightedNarrates
text:
Henry Cavill puts his heart and soul into the roles he plays and genuinely wants to pay tribute to the source material and elevate them to the audience's standards. Yet, despite this, the people he works with either ignore or undermine him. Then blame him because they ignored what he has said. Why bother hiring someone to portray a role if you are going to completely dismiss what they have to say?

I say this at least to Henry: thank you as a person who likes your work for fighting for the fans every chance you had. I think we as a whole are terribly sorry that those who thought they knew better ignored you when you were and still are more connected to the source material than they will ever be.

You tried your hardest for years with both DC and Witcher, but they simply refused to listen and pushed their own agenda, which was not faithful to the characters. You did your best, and we can only be thankful for that. So I salute you, sir!

author: @Dreadlk
text:
They should have just learned from the failure of "Spartacus" and not bothered doing another season.
Once the Fans get attached to a lead character you cannot just replace them with someone else.
 
About the only thing that I have ever seen work in these situations is to create a plot in which the main character is captured and imprisoned.  Then you can make a couple of Season with the new guy searching for him with his old cast.   If the show lasts then you have a final season where they free the original character and both him and the new one fight together until they part ways.

author: @mariuszcerta1927
text: Literary works should be legally protected against interpretations and distortions of the world they depict. Currently, copyright protects the work, but does not restrict adaptations, especially if the author has sold the rights to film it. The screenwriters of The Witcher have repeatedly changed the plot, character motivations, and events, making the series less and less like the literary original. They have even changed the conjunctions of the spheres. What's more, they should be protected from the authors themselves who allow adaptations and interpretations, such as Sapkowski, who is simply old and doesn't care. They should be protected as works of art for generations to come.
